note: SHA-256 sealed predictions. Hash = public commitment. Plaintext is revealed at resolution. Calibration = the singular number that matters at each horizon.

predictions.thesis_public: Cumulative unique-IP views on aureliex.com + aureliex.com during the drop window 2026-04-20T04:20:00-04:00 → 2026-04-20T16:20:00-04:00 will exceed the target.
predictions.calibration_metric: view growth rate from baseline (the singular number that matters)

forfeit.reason: plaintext lost — the exact bytes that produced the committed sha-256 were not stored in a recoverable location. honest disclosure, published here as part of the record.
forfeit.consequence: sp-001 cannot be revealed. the hash stands as unverifiable commitment. this counts as a miscalibration (status: forfeit) for purposes of panel scoring, and the procedural failure itself is logged so sp-002+ are handled differently.
forfeit.fix_for_future: see docs/SEALED_PREDICTIONS.md — from sp-002 onward, plaintext is stored in an encrypted backup committed to the private config, sha-256 verified at seal time, and SEALED_PREDICTION_PLAINTEXT_RAW env vars are provisioned on vercel at sealing time (not at resolution).

predictions.reveal_method: GET /api/reveal — now returns { status: 'forfeited' } for this prediction
predictions.next_sha_trigger: sp-002 will be sealed using the new runbook. the miscalibration is folded in as a procedural failure, not a thesis failure.
